title: Cozy Juicy Citrus Stir-fry
description: A dairy-free-friendly, citrusy and juicy Thai-inspired dish featuring bacon lardon, ready in no time with a pressure cooker.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons soy sauce (or tamari for gluten-free option)
1/2 cup chopped fresh cilantro (cilantro lime juice for garnish if desired)
1 can (15 oz) mandarin oranges, drained and halved
1/4 cup finely diced red bell pepper
ingredients.notes: any color will work for a more vibrant dish

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: high smoke point to prevent burning

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup thinly sliced bacon lardon (or substitute with tofu or tempeh for a vegetarian option)

instructions: Preheat your pressure cooker according to manufacturer’s instructions.
In the bottom of the hot pot, heat oil over medium-high heat.
Add onion, garlic, ginger, and red bell pepper. Stir-fry for about 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add baby carrots, orange halves, soy sauce, and black pepper (if using). Stir to combine all ingredients.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for an additional minute.
Add bacon lardon or substitute to the pot. Ensure it is evenly distributed in the pot.
Lock the lid on and pressure cook at high pressure for 10 minutes, following your cooker’s specific instructions after reaching full power (usually a few more minutes).
Let the pressure release naturally according to manufacturer’s instructions (this will take about 15-20 minutes), then carefully open the lid.
Fold in cilantro if using and mix well. Serve hot with steamed jasmine rice or as a side dish for your next meal.
Garnish with fresh citrus juice from the mandarin oranges, if desired.
